title: grindelia hirsutula subsp. rubricaulis
intro: Grindelia hirsutula subsp. rubricaulis, also known as a plant in the aster family, is a type of flowering plant native to North America. The medicinal properties of Grindelia hirsutula subsp. rubricaulis lie in its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. Grindelia hirsutula subsp. rubricaulis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical ointment to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
